danger_signals.visual_form: Unit sprites moving across map toward player settlements
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to resources and infrastructure

danger_signals.visual_form: Depleted tree tiles and resource nodes disappearing from terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Finite map resources create strategic scarcity and vulnerability

danger_signals.visual_form: Unfinished walls and towers in construction state
danger_signals.meaning: Settlement exposed to attack during development phases

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy units with visually improved armor/weaponry not yet available to player

safety_signals.visual_form: Connected defensive wall structures surrounding settlement
Dense clusters of trees, stone deposits, and gold veins on map
safety_signals.meaning: Economic stability and room for expansion

safety_signals.visual_form: Upgraded unit models and buildings reflecting higher technological tier
safety_signals.meaning: Access to superior military and economic technologies

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple military unit groups positioned around settlements
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive capability and deterrent to aggression

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct player color assignment (blue/red/yellow/cyan/etc. depending on player slot)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Egyptian architectural style in buildings (pyramidal structures, distinctive monuments)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy Egyptian units use same architectural style but identified by opposing player color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Town centers and monuments styled with Egyptian aesthetic; Angkor Wat and similar civilization-specific wonders

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Greek architectural style in buildings (columned structures, classical design)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Greek units maintain style but differ by player color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Greek-styled temples, barracks, and defensive structures

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mesopotamian style units identified by opposing player color

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: East Asian architectural style (pagodas, distinctive roofing)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: East Asian styled units in enemy player color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: East Asian-styled town centers and military buildings

faction_indicators.faction: Other 8 civilizations (Assyrians, Choson, Hittites, Minoans, Persians, Phoenicians, Shang, Sumerians)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distributed across four architectural style groups (East Asian, Mesopotamian, Egyptian, Greek)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Civilization identity maintained across player colors via architectural style
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Civilization-specific building aesthetics and unit designs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Basic primitive buildings with minimal visual detail; hunter-gatherer themed architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited unit types available; slower resource gathering; no advanced military units

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Buildings gain more detail and complexity; improved architectural representation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New unit types unlock; resource gathering efficiency improves; basic military units become available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Further refined building aesthetics; more sophisticated fortification structures visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Advanced military units available; improved armor and weapons visible on unit sprites; economic technologies enhance gathering

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Most refined and detailed buildings; peak architectural representation for civilization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Full military roster available; superior unit stats; access to all civilization-specific technologies and units

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy units actively moving across shared map; fortifications showing damage or wear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Military units engage in combat; defensive structures activate; resource gathering becomes risky in exposed areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy units stationary or patrol patterns; no active combat animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Workers gather resources safely; economic development prioritized; military units may be relegated to defensive positions

environmental_storytelling.example: As civilization advances through ages, the visual quality and complexity of buildings increases, showing technological and cultural advancement without explicit text
Campaign scenarios feature civilization-specific wonders (Angkor Wat for Khmer context) as visual objectives that communicate cultural identity and historical significance
Depleted terrain tiles where trees have been cut down or stone/gold extracted communicate permanent resource consumption and strategic map control importance
Concentration of walls, towers, and defensive structures visually communicates strategic importance of locations and player investment in defense
Grouping of different military unit types near enemy settlements visually communicates threat level and technological sophistication of opposing force
Specific map layouts and objectives (Egyptian, Greek, Babylonian, Yamato campaigns) communicate historical narratives and civilization-specific challenges without cutscenes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Becomes focal point of unit production and age advancement; visual indicator of civilization development priority
Units disappear into structure, indicating defensive preparation and fortification readiness
Move toward enemy units with specific animation pattern communicating religious influence mechanic
Repetitive pathfinding to resource nodes and back to storage communicates economic activity and supply chain
Stationary positioning suggests defensive stance and readiness; clustering communicates army strength
Sudden appearance of upgraded unit visuals and new building types communicates technological leap and shifting power dynamics
Partially rendered structure with visual building animation communicates vulnerability and ongoing commitment of resources

notes: Age of Empires uses a color-coded player identification system layered over civilization-specific architectural styles to distinguish factions. The four ages serve as the primary world-state manifestation system, with visual upgrades to unit sprites and building detail reflecting technological progression. Danger and safety signals are primarily environmental (resource availability, fortification completeness) rather than explicit UI elements. The game communicates political/military situations through unit positioning, fortification density, and technological parity visualization. Campaign scenarios provide historical context and civilization-specific identity through architectural theming and narrative objectives.
